panels.title: Publish, Subscribe, API, and Node Health
Disconnect Reasons
panels.description: Count of participant close events grouped by LiveKit DisconnectReason over the dashboard time range. CLIENT_INITIATED / DUPLICATE_IDENTITY are benign; SIGNAL_CLOSE / JOIN_FAILURE / ICE failures point at the client's network; SERVER_SHUTDOWN / MIGRATION point at this LiveKit node. (Excludes connection-quality scorer logs, which also carry a 'reason' field like 'packet' and are NOT disconnects.)

panels.title: Disconnects by Reason
panels.description: Same DisconnectReason breakdown over time. A spike in CLIENT_INITIATED / SIGNAL_CLOSE confined to a few series is client-side; a wall of SERVER_SHUTDOWN/MIGRATION across everyone lines up with a restart annotation = server-side.

panels.title: ICE / Connection Failures
panels.description: Lines indicating media-path trouble: ICE failed/disconnected, DTLS errors, failed negotiation, unresponsive participants. Almost always the client's network or NAT rather than the server.

panels.title: Errors & Warnings
panels.description: LiveKit log volume at warn/error level. A jump here that coincides with disconnects is a strong server-side signal.

panels.title: Disconnect / Reconnect Events
panels.description: Raw LiveKit log lines for participant close, reconnect/resume, ICE failures and unresponsive participants. Read the line behind any spike above.

panels.title: Disconnects by Participant
panels.description: Participant close events grouped by Matrix identity, room and DisconnectReason over the time range. A single identity with many ICE/SIGNAL rows is a repeat offender (their network); spread across everyone points at this node. Identity is the LiveKit participant = Matrix user/device. (Excludes scorer quality logs so only real disconnects are counted.)

targets.expr: sum by (participant, room, reason) (count_over_time({job="livekit"} | json | participant != "" | reason != "" | caller !~ "connectionquality" [$__range]))

panels.title: ICE / candidate selection (who · IP · transport)
panels.description: Raw lines for ICE connection + selected candidate pair. The selected-pair line carries the participant's real media IP and transport (udp = direct, tcp/relay = NAT fallback / needs TURN). Read the IP/transport from the line; once the exact JSON key is confirmed (README Discovery) this can become parsed columns.

panels.title: Connection Quality Score by Participant
panels.description: Per-participant connection quality score (0 = lost … 1 = excellent) from the hidden-bot exporter (job=livekit-quality). The line that consistently sits low is the friend who is dropping; correlate the timestamp with the ICE/IP panel above. No data until the exporter is deployed.

panels.title: Worst participants now
panels.description: Lowest current quality scores across active rooms (bottom 15). Quick 'who is bad right now' view during a live call. No data until the exporter is deployed.

panels.title: Per-participant media detail (from LiveKit quality scorer, debug logs)
Connection score (0-100) by participant · direction
panels.description: Worst-track connection score per participant and direction, from LiveKit's scorer. down = what THEY receive (SFU→them, 'can they hear us'); up = THEM→SFU (their mic reaching us, 'can we hear them'). 100=excellent. A line diving toward 0 is the exact person + direction that degraded.

targets.expr: min by (ident, dir) (min_over_time({job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", cl="caller", val="score" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | unwrap val [1m]))

panels.title: Packets lost / min by participant · direction
panels.description: Total RTP packets lost per minute (summed across a participant's tracks), from the scorer stat window. Non-zero on a friend's up direction = their mic audio is losing packets before it even reaches us. down = they're losing what we send them.

targets.expr: sum by (ident, dir) (sum_over_time({job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", cl="caller", val="stat.packetsLost" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | unwrap val [1m]))

panels.title: Jitter (max, raw µs) by participant · direction
panels.description: Max jitter reported in the scorer stat window (LiveKit reports jitter in microseconds). Spikes correlate with choppy/robotic audio. Split by direction to see whose path and which way.

targets.expr: max by (ident, dir) (max_over_time({job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", cl="caller", val="stat.jitterMax" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | unwrap val [1m]))

panels.title: RTT (max, ms) by participant · direction
panels.description: Max round-trip time in the scorer stat window (ms). High or climbing RTT = latency/congestion on that participant's path.

targets.expr: max by (ident, dir) (max_over_time({job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", cl="caller", val="stat.rttMax" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | unwrap val [1m]))

panels.title: Score breakdown now (loss / resolution / bitrate)
panels.description: Latest score components per participant·direction. packetScore low = packet loss; layerScore low = forced to a lower resolution layer; bitrateScore low = not enough bandwidth. Tells you WHY the score dropped, not just that it did.

targets.expr: min by (ident, dir) (last_over_time({job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", cl="caller", val="score" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | unwrap val [$__range]))
min by (ident, dir) (last_over_time({job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", cl="caller", val="packetScore" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | unwrap val [$__range]))
min by (ident, dir) (last_over_time({job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", cl="caller", val="layerScore" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | unwrap val [$__range]))
min by (ident, dir) (last_over_time({job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", cl="caller", val="bitrateScore" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | unwrap val [$__range]))

panels.title: Degraded quality moments (who · direction · numbers)
panels.description: Every scorer sample where quality is NOT excellent, one line each: participant, direction, quality, score and the raw loss/jitter/rtt. This is the 'exactly what happened and when' feed — when a friend says they cut out, find their identity here at that time.

targets.expr: {job="livekit"} | json ident="participant", dir="direction", q="quality", sc="score", lost="stat.packetsLost", jit="stat.jitterMax", rtt="stat.rttMax", cl="caller" | cl=~"connectionquality/scorer.go.*" | q!="EXCELLENT" | line_format "{{.ident}} [{{.dir}}] quality={{.q}} score={{.sc}} lost={{.lost}} jitterUs={{.jit}} rttMs={{.rtt}}"
